title: phacelia cumingii
intro: Phacelia cumingii, also known as Fijian scorpionweed, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phacelia cumingii include relief from respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Phacelia cumingii can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it has been used in traditional medicine in the Pacific Islands for centuries.
